page: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a GPU-accelerated virtual server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features 48 vCPUs mapped from 24 physical Intel x86_64 cores running at 2.0 GHz with shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 496.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.33 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it bundles eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, delivering a combined 128 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1500 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results show strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 73907.77 and a PassMark CPU score of 55603.55, placing it in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly capable, registering a strong read-only small block cached speed of 4709.75 GB/sec and a large block write speed of 101.16 GB/sec. In database operations, the server achieves a strong Redis SET throughput of 74.78 million operations per second. For language model workloads, the hardware achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds, reaching 8182.48 tokens per second on a small 135M model and 610.38 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

The shared CPU allocation and 2.0 GHz clock speed result in average single-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 1781.09. Additionally, the processor lacks AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets, which may limit certain CPU-bound matrix operations. However, the high density of eight NVIDIA A16 GPUs and 496.0 GB of RAM offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that prioritize parallel GPU processing over raw single-threaded CPU speed. This server is suited for multi-stream video transcoding, remote graphics rendering, medium-scale LLM inference, and parallelized machine learning workloads.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 48 shared Intel vCPUs, 496.0 GB of RAM, and a 1500 GB NVMe SSD. It features eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing a total of 128 GB of VRAM. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 55603.55 and top-tier LLM prompt processing speeds, such as 610.38 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. However, single-core performance remains average due to the shared CPU allocation and 2.0 GHz clock speed. This hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized GPU tasks rather than single-threaded CPU operations. It is suited for video transcoding, remote desktop hosting, and medium-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram is a GPU-accelerated server with 48 Intel vCPUs, 496 GB RAM, 1500 GB NVMe storage, and eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs delivering 128 GB of total VRAM.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-48c-496g-128vram features 48 Intel vCPUs, 496 GB RAM, 1500 GB NVMe SSD, and eight NVIDIA A16 GPUs with 128 GB total VRAM.
tagline: A high-density GPU server featuring eight NVIDIA Ampere GPUs, massive system memory, and NVMe storage for parallelized acceleration workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 128 GB total VRAM
48 shared Intel vCPUs and 496.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs
Strong multi-core CPU and memory write bandwidth performance
Suited for video transcoding, remote rendering, and machine learning inference
